description_long: This is a rustic stone water well encircled by a low, roughly cylindrical wall made of uneven, moss-covered stones. Two tall, twisted wooden posts rise from either side of the well, their bark gnarled and partially stripped, supporting a horizontal wooden beam. Attached to the beam is a simple crank mechanism with a handle and a spindle, from which a rope descends into the well's dark interior. The well sits on a circular patch of earth, with scattered stones and patches of grass suggesting age and disuse. The overall appearance is weathered and natural, evoking a sense of history and rural charm.
description: A weathered stone water well with twisted wooden supports, a crank mechanism, and mossy details, set on a circular patch of earth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full stone well, both twisted wooden posts, the crank handle, and the rope descending into the well.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the left post and the crank mechanism, with the well's opening partially obscured.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the mossy stone wall, both posts, and the horizontal beam, with the crank handle visible on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the twisted form of the right post and the open well interior, with the crank handle less visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A simple crank and spindle mechanism with a rope is present, made of wood and metal, mounted on the horizontal beam.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low stone wall approximately 0.7 meters high.
